entry.title: Goods movement of errors because of account assignments
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have had problem posting goods issues for any components to any process/production orders in DEV system since yesterday and we don’t know the reason that has caused this. For every goods issue, we get an error where account assignment to the GL account is missing. The components we are working with are based on standard valuation classes.   Our problem is related to the goods receipt  and the goods issue  when we try to create a booking of costs and accounts related to manufacturing towards a production order or a process order.

According to our finance colleagues the account assignment should be passed from the Production order and not a cost center, our order is the cost object.

Attachments:


 

We have the same problem for all accounts related to production.

So I tried with the following since we have a ticket to SAP:

Update default account assignment = no solution

Update automatic account determination = no solution

Check of the field status if the account requires a mandatory CO Object = no solution

Can someone please help me out here, someone that maybe experienced the same kind of issue?

entry.title: Creating a “My Team-esque” Story Report for HR Managers + Deep Links
entry.content:
Introduction:

  • Currently SuccessFactors does not have a “My Team” section for HR Managers like it does for Managers/Supervisors.
  • The aim of this blog is to bridge that gap by creating a dashboard in people analytics.
  • By using the dashboard, HR Managers will easily be able to see folks that are tagged to them.
  • The dashboard also leverages deep-links to that they will be able to jump to the live profile of the employee as well as specific portlets within the profile.
  • After creating the dashboard, a deep link to the dashboard itself can be created and linked on external systems like Work Zone, Share Point, Intranet Sites Etc.
  • The report is contextual, as in it will automatically show only users tagged to the logged in HR Manager (and respects RBP like all other reports)

 

Step 1: Creating the query.

The first step is building out the query in query designer.

1) Add Tables:

Based on your requirements, add the tables needed. For the purpose of this blog, I have kept the query rather simple.

  • Personal information Table
  • Job Information Table

2) Create a scope filter:

Click on the Create Filter button in the nav bar -> new-filter -> Scope Filtering -> Job Information

Under “Start with” Select Human Resource (You could also select Manager or other relationships based on your requirements and business use case)

Under “Who should be the subject of this report?” select the name of any HR Manager for testing, however once you are done testing, switch it to Logged in User

 

3) Save the query:

You can now save the query and click on Finish to jump into the story designer page

 

Step 2: Design The Dashboard

1) Select a layout

You can either design the dashboard from scratch or use a layout. I used the ‘Title Page Report’ Layout and redesigned accordingly.

2) Create required pages

Duplicate and Create additional based on the number of reports you want to have. Example

Home: This will be the base page as seen in the screenshot + deeplinks to the employee profile

Personal Information: This will have personal information data + deeplinks to personal info portlet

Job Information: This will have job info data + deeplinks to job information portlet

 

Step 3: Creating the home page

 

1) Create a Cross-Tab table and add in the required fields.

Example: User Id, Username, Name, Division, Department Etc

2) Below the table add text, and use the designer to adjust the size/look of the text boxes

 

Step 4: Creating deep links for employees’ profile on the home page of the dashboard

1) Right click on the userid column -> Table Function-> Add-> Hyperlink

2) Add the following details:

 

The External URL would be: https://performancemanager4.successfactors.com/sf/liveprofile?selecteduser= User ID

(Please adjust URL based on data centre/instance/environment )

When inputting the external URL, the User ID bit needs to be selected:

3) After saving you would be able to click on the user ids to jump to the employees profile

 

Step 5: Adding links to the text boxes

The next step is adding links to the text boxes below the report on the home page.

1) Right Click on the text-> Add->Hyperlink

2) Enter the details as required.

3) After saving you would be able to click on the text box to navigate to the Personal information page created in Step 2

 

Step 6: Adding a deep link on the Job Information Page

1) After creating a Cross-Tab Table on the Job information page, follow Step 4 to add a deep link

2) However, for the External URL use the following:

https://performancemanager4.successfactors.com/sf/liveprofile?blockType=JOB_INFO_BLOCK&selecteduser=User  ID

(Please adjust URL based on data center/instance/environment )

3) After saving, when you click on user id, it will directly take you to the Job Information Portlet of the employee

 

Step 7: Repeat Step 6 for Other Pages and Portlets

You can then create multiple pages to show data from different portlets and then deep link to those specific portlets

Example: Personal Information, Job Information, Compensation, Emergency Contact, Custom Portlets Etc

You have successfully created a My Team Inspired Dashboard For HR Managers!

Pro Tips:

  • You can create personalized greetings- Hello “Name”, Welcome to your HRBP Dashboard (Right click on text-> Dynamic Text -> Name)
  • You can add icons, images and texts to beautify the dashboard
  • You can add a Back Button Image, and link it back to the home page of the dashboard
  • For more information on deep links please take a look at my blog: Deep linking to SAP SuccessFactors Standard Portlets and Custom MDF portlets
  • For more details on Deep Links in story reports, check out this KBA
  • After the story is ready, from report center you can generate a deep link to the story which can be placed in other systems like Work Zone

entry.title: Re: Additional depreciation area and second migration
entry.content:
Hi to all,

here the answer from SAP:

Solution provided:
Dear Mr. Ganz,
actually, it is a quite unusual approach to use ABLDT successively for partial transfer of legacy values per ledger group.
However, from technical perspective this is possible as long as these separate transfers are being executed within the same legacy data transfer segment in FAA_CMP.
In such a scenario the legacy values of the depreciation areas which are assigned to different ledger groups all refer to the same legacy transfer date stored in ANLA-LDT_DATE, which is correct.
However, this does not apply in case of multiple value transfers to the same fixed asset as part of different legacy data transfer dates in FAA_CMP.
Best regards,
Marco Herhuth
Application Innovation Services – AIS, Customer Solution Support and Innovation | Asset Accounting
SAP Development Support

entry.title: Terraform Cloud Foundry Provider for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introducing Cloud Foundry Terraform Provider for SAP BTP !

With the increasing adoption of the SAP Business Technology Platform and the popularity of the  Cloud Foundry runtime environment, one of the most common requests was around a terraform provider for Cloud Foundry.  Keeping this  in mind we are happy to announce the first release v0.1.0-beta of the SAP Terraform provider for Cloud Foundry.

Motivation

While it is true that a community version provider exists, the following aspects led us to decide to bring in a new terraform provider:

  • Unlock capabilities in Cloud Foundry that are only provided by the V3 APIs of Cloud Foundry.
  • With the deprecation of V2 APIs, we need to ensure that all resources and data sources are based on the V3 API schema of the Cloud Foundry V3 specification.
  • An updated terraform provider that uses the latest Terraform Plugin Framework and the actively maintained native go-client.
  • While being generic, ensuring compatibility and feature focus on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

What’s covered in Beta?

The beta version focuses on the main features offered by the Cloud Foundry environment on the Business Technology Platform and requirements of our customers. The main list of resources and data sources added are:

  • Space
  • Space Quota
  • Space Role
  •  Application
  • Route
  • Service Instance
  • Service Credential Binding

Generic Cloud Foundry objects are also being slowly introduced. Head over to the terraform registry for a complete list of resources and data-sources.

Being a beta, we expect rough edges and corner case issues. Therefore, we do not recommend using this productively yet.

Our Ask: Please try and give your feedback

Head over to our registry and try it out today!  Create feature requests and bug reports in the issues section of the Github Repository.

For questions and discussions start or join a discussion and interact with the team behind the development and other users.

Looking forward to knowing about your experiences.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Connector

Serves as a link between SAP BTP applications and on-premise systems.
It can connect Cloud to On-Premise and On-premise to Cloud.

SAP Cloud ALM

SAP Cloud ALM provides Landscape Management Services which is an application that allows the connection of Supported Cloud Services and On Premise Systems to support the various use cases that SAP Cloud ALM provides. This is the list of Supported Solutions, which is continuously updated and the setup and configuration of those systems can be found in the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert under Setup Managed Components (Services & Systems).

There are 3 type of Connections possible in SAP Cloud ALM Landscape Management.

  1. Subscribed
  2. Registered
  3. Manual

Subscribed are Subscription Cloud Services that can be read automatically from the SAP Support Backbone via a job called SLIS.

Registered are Cloud Services or On-Premise Systems that will have a registration Program/Setup that is performed from the Managed Service or System. Some examples are for On-Premise ABAP systems like SAP Business Suite or S/4HANA On-Premise use a program “/SDF/CALM_SETUP, S/4HANA Cloud uses a defined Communication System and a specific Communication Arrangement Template, while SuccessFactors uses the Integration Service Registration Center, and selectn SAP Cloud ALM.

Manual are for very specific use cases where the service is not subscribed or can it be registered.
Currently there are only 3 Manual type of connections and all of them require SAP Cloud Connector.

1) SAP Cloud Connector can be connected to SAP Cloud ALM and there is one use case. Health Monitoring. Setup for that is found here.

2) SAP Process Integration (Java) can be connected to SAP Cloud ALM. The Cloud Connector is used because this on-premise system type does not have ST-PI as ABAP on-premise systems do, si it is used to collect the data. The setup for an SAP PI Java system is here.

3) SAP Focused Run can use SAP Cloud connector as a reverse proxy. In this solution SAP Cloud ALM together with an Cloud Connector act as reverse proxy for SAP Focused Run and allow a secure transfer of pushed monitoring data to SAP Focused Run. The setup for this can be found here.

 

These 3 use cases are the only use cases for SAP Cloud Connector. No other system/service can be connected to SAP Cloud ALM via SAP Cloud Connector (SCC). If you try to use SCC to connect any other System or Service it will not be successful. Again, the setup for supported Services and Systems can be found here.

Need Help?

If you are having issues configuring the SAP Cloud Connector you can get help on component BC-MID-SCC
If the Cloud Connector is setup and working but you cannot get one of the Supported Use Cases working in SAP Cloud ALM you can get help on SV-CLM-INF-CON

entry.title: Can’t Miss Asset and Service Management Sessions at SAP Sapphire 2024 in Orlando
entry.content:
Bring Out the Best in Your Business ─ Can’t Miss Asset and Service Management Sessions at SAP Sapphire 2024 in Orlando 

Don't miss out on the perfect opportunity for an ultimate experience at SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference in Orlando. We are excited to share some details about the Asset and Service Management presence at the three-day event in Orlando, Florida, on June 3-5.

Check out the list of can’t-miss Asset and Service Management sessions we’ve compiled below:

ASUG862 | Realize Your Supply Chain Potential with SAP Business AI

Monday, June 3 | 08:00 a.m.

Although AI is not new to supply chains, the amount of progress and innovation over the past few years has been enormous in terms of what is now practical and available. This seminar will cover the integration of AI in SAP’s Digital Supply Chain solutions based on our three core principles: Relevant, Reliable, Responsible. Attendees will gain access to experts and see firsthand how to synchronize operations and maximize efficiency with predictive maintenance, plan customer demand reliably with AI-powered demand forecasting and improve quality with intelligent anomaly detection through visual inspection with SAP Joule.

IND100 | AI: The massive potential for manufacturing and asset-intensive industries

Tuesday, June 04 | 10:30 a.m.

AI is transforming manufacturing industries. Use cases for predictive analytics, personalization, and smart insights are driving opportunities from planning and maintenance to sales and supply chain. Discuss the value of embedded AI, its impact on build versus buy decisions, and the range of AI scenarios embedded in SAP software business processes.

BTP219 | A plant maintenance journey with SAP Datasphere and SAP Data Intelligence

Tuesday, June 04 | 11:30 a.m.

Hear how sugar refining company Florida Crystals Corporation is leveraging the SAP Datasphere and SAP Data Intelligence solutions to improve plant maintenance. Explore the SAP Build Apps tool and learn how to integrate real-time machine values with plant maintenance data and engage with your operations team for a smooth project.

SCM110 | Supercharge field service management operations with AI

Tuesday, June 04 | 01:30 p.m.

Discover the future of intelligent scheduling with a demo showcase of our AI-based scheduling functionality. You can also get practical insights on how to maximize the benefits of AI in your field service management processes.

SCM146 | Owens Corning scales asset condition monitoring

Tuesday, June 04 | 02:30 p.m.

Join Owens Corning to learn about its innovative approach to scaling equipment condition monitoring. The global building and construction materials leader used standard information models, remote monitoring, and reliability-as-a-service solutions to drive improvements in asset performance across all manufacturing sites.

DIS130 | SAP runs SAP: Asset management and analytics in an immersive experience

Tuesday, June 04 | 02:30 p.m.

Embark on a virtual journey to SAP’s own power plant, and gain real-world insights into how SAP uses our own asset management solutions. In an immersive experience, explore the maintenance stage of an end-to-end asset management process, gleaning real-time insights to maintenance and performance and enabling analytics for intelligent decisions.

IND236 | Renewables: Staying ahead in a dynamic and evolving industry

Tuesday, June 04 | 02:30 p.m.

Thrive in the renewable energy sector with outstanding visibility and insights aided by SAP solutions. Optimized core business functions spanning engineering, land acquisition, spend management, supply chain, people management, projects, asset management, and operations are vital to success.

SCM149 | Unleash your potential with enterprise-wide adoption of cloud solutions

Wednesday, June 05 | 10:30 a.m.

Freeport McMoRan Inc. highlights the importance of software adoption and change management in driving organizational success. Hear from the mining company how SAP software solutions can enhance productivity, streamline processes, and improve decision-making capabilities, and how a proactive approach towards software adoption helps you stay ahead.

DIS130 | SAP runs SAP: Asset management and analytics in an immersive experience

Wednesday, June 05 | 10:30 a.m.

Embark on a virtual journey to SAP’s own power plant, and gain real-world insights into how SAP uses our own asset management solutions. In an immersive experience, explore the maintenance stage of an end-to-end asset management process, gleaning real-time insights to maintenance and performance and enabling analytics for intelligent decisions.

SCM151 | HECO's journey towards operational excellence

Wednesday, June 05 | 11:00 a.m.

Hawaiian Electric Company Inc. (HECO) took a transformative journey to get the most out of its existing SAP landscape and modernize its software and business processes. Learn about HECO’s need to use a new similarity measure, the journey from software selection through implementation, project successes and challenges, and what’s next.

SCM109 | Strategy and road map for asset and service management

Wednesday, June 05 | 11:00 a.m.

Hear about market and customer priorities for asset and service management. We review SAP's strategy and road map, see recent innovations, and look at innovation priorities for 2024 and beyond for both SAP S/4HANA and our cloud extensions for asset operators and equipment service organizations.

SBN210 | Improve plant operations with SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

Wednesday, June 05 | 11:30 a.m.

Experience the power of collaboration with a central asset repository and SAP S/4HANA. Unite your assets in one central location, streamlining your asset management process. With real-time data updates and tight integration, you'll have the tools to make smarter, faster decisions. Say hello to a new era of efficiency and productivity.

SCM131 | Entegris innovates for paperless plant operations

Wednesday, June 05 | 01:00 p.m.

Learn about Entegris' plant modernization journey, transitioning from paper to tablet-integrated SAP processes. We look at how the manufacturer enhanced competitiveness through scoping, design, agile delivery, feedback, and testing. We also explore methodologies that helped transform processes to deliver competitive advantage.

SCM132 | Transforming field service management operations at VINCI Energies

Wednesday, June 05 | 01:00 p.m.

Discover how VINCI Energies transformed field service into a strategic asset. With the SAP Field Service Management solution, the energy company streamlined operations across 10 countries and 101 business units, and empowered over 5,000 users.

SCM107 | Revolutionize asset performance with IoT and AI-enabled processes

Wednesday, June 05 | 01:30 p.m.

Learn more about how the SAP Asset Performance Management application uses the latest IoT and AI technologies in a demo showcase. You can also gain practical insights on how customers use these capabilities to optimize predictive maintenance and inspection processes.

SCM153 | Transforming asset and reliability management at Chevron

Wednesday, June 05 | 02:00 p.m.

Hear how energy company Chevron Corporation is advancing its asset integrity and reliability management with SAP S/4HANA and the SAP Asset Performance Management application. We consider how the energy company is standardizing business processes and master data across its operations and explore key project phases and the vision for the future.

117 | Asset and service management solutions from SAP

Demo Station | Open During Show Floor Hours

Maximize asset performance, reduce costs, and deliver maintenance and service excellence with our asset and service management solutions. Integrated, closed-loop processes and AI-enabled technologies offered by the solutions help you optimize your asset and service management operations.

105 | RISE with SAP: Asset and service management  

Demo Station | Open During Show Floor Hours

Find out how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition differs from the classic SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) in asset management (plant maintenance) and service management (customer management). Discuss how new features can enhance your business processes in these domains while leveraging your existing knowledge, skills, and investment.

 

Start putting together your personal agenda today. Check out the full session catalog here.

Stay updated on all of the latest announcements by following us on LinkedIn SAP Supply Chain Management.

entry.title: s4hc Public,How to configure different tax accounts (100297) for different scenarios,
Big Update on the e-learning “Integrating SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud with SAP S/4HANA"
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

This is Felix from the CRM & CX Product Learning team. With this blog post, I want to inform you about a big update we recently published for the e-learning “Integrating SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud with SAP S/4HANA” and share some insights.

 

Some Background

Although SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 is becoming more and more relevant these days, we are still paying attention to existing courses and reviewing learner’s feedback regularly.

Two years ago, we published the e-learning course “Integrating SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud with SAP S/4HANA” which was based on the classroom training “SAP Sales and Service Cloud Integration with On-Premise SAP Solutions” (course code C4H450, formerly known as C4C50).

The overview page of the updated course.

With this e-learning, we made some content of this originally 5-day classroom training available for self-paced learning. The initial release mainly comprised theory and demos for master data scenarios. However, the content didn’t quite meet the expectations of several learners. We received feedback such as “too superficial” or “too broad-ranging” and some audio/video tracks have room for improvement.

For that reason, the course received a massive content and quality update.
Let me share some insights:

 

General Updates

  • “Click Animations” across the whole course have been replaced with alternative media (text, graphics, videos)
  • Many videos have been replaced with versions that provide better quality and more explanations (especially in units 3, 4, and 6).
  • Most of the videos are accompanied by a textual summary providing the key takeaways for easier recapitulation, better supporting different learner types, and finding information quicker when searching the page.

"Click Animations" have been replaced with other media, such as images or videos.

Video quality has been improved. Most of the videos are accompanied by a textual summary providing the key takeaways.

 

Content Updates per Unit

  • Content of units 1 and 2 has been rearranged and made a bit crisper.
  • Unit 2 (Getting to know the Systems and the System Landscape) now briefly introduces each system to help learners who haven’t worked with a particular system yet. The S/4 lesson contains a summary of the most relevant transactions that can be used for reference.
  • Unit 3 (Examining Technical Connectivity and Security Aspects) lays the foundation for technical connectivity and essential security principles, such as encryption and digital certificates. This challenging theory topic has been enhanced and is now accompanied by two new videos, and also mentions how digital certificates are managed inside and outside of SAP systems.
  • Unit 4 (Configuring the Systems) has been enhanced with extensive explanations and updated videos. Moreover, configuration steps for SAP S/HANA have been added, which were not in place in the initial release.
  • Unit 6 (Monitoring and Troubleshooting) has been reworked entirely, including updated videos.
  • Unit 7 (Running Transactional Data Scenarios) was introduced in the last update about one year ago because the initial release didn’t contain transactional scenarios. This unit has received minor updates, such as textual key takeaways for the configuration videos, which have been added to make it easier to follow them in your own system landscape.

Example for the content improvements: Unit 3 (Examining Technical Connectivity and Security Aspects) now contains new videos.

Example for the content improvements: Unit 4 (Configuring the Systems) now contains configuration steps for SAP S/4HANA.

Sounds interesting? ➡️ Visit the e-learning.

 

Changes in Product Names

Another part of your feedback was about using different names for a single solution. Some products have been renamed several times in the past. Especially the split of “SAP Cloud for Customer” into “SAP Sales Cloud” and “SAP Service Cloud” often causes confusion, as these names refer to specific solution names on the one hand but also solution portfolios, including other solutions on the other hand. Finally, in this course, we refer to foundation topics that are still shared between them, so we refer to “SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud” which most people merge into “SAP Sales and Service Cloud”.

While we try to use the combined variant “SAP Sales and Service Cloud” due to better readability as much as possible, you’ll still find the separate variant or even older names and abbreviations in some places, especially in links or technical terms, such as the Cloud Integration package name “SAPHybrisCloudforCustomer­Integration­with­SAPS4HANA”, or table and transactions containing “C4C” or “COD”. These can’t be changed without breaking compatibility. Hence, we have to live with it.

 

Proficiency Level and Prerequisites

Although this course is a beginner learning (the classroom training has associate level), it has a certain complexity. Three major SAP products are involved that learners might be more or less familiar with. We deal mainly with technical topics that ideally require existing knowledge in the respective area.

So, we still face the challenge of balancing between different detail levels and concentrating on the basics. Addressing potential challenges of customer projects would be a topic for intermediate learning.

While giving a general overview and introduction to the integration topic, we show the general configuration steps and explain why they are needed. However, the e-learning doesn’t contain a demonstration for all settings. A good example of this approach is the code list mapping, where a sample code list is used for explanation while mentioning all specialties of each and every code list would be too much for the training.

 

Conclusion

The good thing about e-learning is that you can go at your own pace and skip a topic if you’re already familiar with it 😉

I hope you enjoy the updated version of the e-learning!

Happy learning!
Felix

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Create an outbound interface to send the data to SFTP-Server from Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

1. Can we export the daily delta datasets to the Non-SAP application or file server in a flat file (CSV or XLS format) via SFTP connection directly from the datasphere view or local table?
2. Can we generate a filename based on timestamp (yyyymmdd_hhmmss)?

Currently, this is possible with BW. We have a process chain where process steps are created with the ABAP program and its variants to generate and transfer the data from a particular DSO to the CFTP server.

Below is the BW process chain screenshot which showcases the file generation and transfer steps (It is using ABAP code with a variant where the variant contains the DSO names and the loading date based on which the delta dataset is created).

Is this possible with SAP Datasphere?

Thanks!

Regards,

Mandar Damle

entry.title: Notes to Documents Migration
entry.content:
Why do we migrate?

The Notes functionality was introduced early in the development of SAP Cloud ALM. However, we have encountered certain limitations with this app that prevent us from offering more advanced document management capabilities. As a result, we launched the Documents application last year, which utilizes SAP Fiori elements and follows different architectural guidelines.

The Documents app has now achieved feature parity with the Notes application and even offers additional capabilities. Going forward, we will only be investing in the Documents app. Therefore, it is time for all Notes users to benefit from the enhanced capabilities provided by the Documents app. We will be migrating all Notes into Documents!

How and when will ‘Notes’ be migrated?

The migration will be carried out automatically within a specific timeframe. All information, tags, references you captured in your Notes as well as the relation to processes will be available on the Documents!

To facilitate a smooth transition, we will first switch the Notes app to "Read-only" mode. This means that you will still be able to view your existing Notes, but you will no longer be able to create new ones or to edit existing ones. If you need to create new documentation, we recommend starting to use the Documents app, where we have introduced a new document type called ‘Note’. The actual migration process will take place over a 48-hour period on June 12th-14th. Once the migration is complete, you will find all your Notes in the Documents app. You can easily filter them by selecting the document type ‘Note’. Your migrated Notes will still be visible in the Notes app, allowing you to compare them with the migrated Documents for assurance.

What do you need to do?

There is no immediate action required from your end. However, we encourage you to consider using the Documents app or familiarising yourself with it by exploring the Help documentation. As you become more acquainted with the additional functionality, you may find new ways to utilise your Notes in the future.

After the completion of the Notes migration on June 14th, you can verify that all your Notes are successfully migrated to the Documents app.

In case you have specific questions, please post your question below or contact us via cloudalm@sap.com.

Benefits

All documentation is then combined in one location: the Documents App! 

Check out some of the new capabilities:

  • Status & Priority
  • Owner
  • Admin data: Created by/on, changed by/on
  • Excel Import
  • Assignment to process hierarchy
  • Relation to Requirements, Tasks, User Stories, Defects
  • API available

Also, in case you have created Notes as part of a scope and you would like to change the scope assignment, you can do this in the future since the scope of Documents can be changed.

And stay tuned for many new features to come…

entry.title: Quantity Distribution
entry.content:
Hello there! 

If you're new to the world of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and Bill of Materials (BOM), one concept that may sound a bit complex initially, but could be tremendously beneficial to your business, is Equal Quantity Distribution. Though it might seem like a mouthful at first, understanding this principle could be the key to unlocking greater efficiency and potential in your production process. 

What is Equal Quantity Distribution? 

Imagine you're planning to host a big party. You have a list (your BOM) of all the ingredients you need for your menu. Now, would it make sense to buy all of your supplies weeks in advance? Probably not. Some ingredients are perishable, others might only be needed at the time of cooking. So, you plan your shopping trips, distributing your purchases over time. 

Similarly, Equal Quantity Distribution can be seen as a "smart shopping" strategy for your production process. In the context of dependent requirements and BOM, Equal Quantity Distribution is a method of scheduling the procurement or production of materials exactly when it's required during the production cycle. The demand is equally dispersed along with your chosen timeline or specific stages of your production process. 

But not only production processes can benefit from Equal Quantity Distribution, also the Product Availability Check (PAC) can incorporate it when determining on which date and in which quantity a requirement (for example, a sales order item) can be fulfilled. 

What does this mean for the supply and demand? 

In a production order the demand for the components is on start date of the operation. With quantity distribution, these demands are distributed across the production runtime. Similarly, the whole supply that is created with the production order is planned at the end of the order. Following diagram depicts this: 

Equal Quantity Distribution changes this by distributing supply and demand, like depicted in following diagram: 

 How can businesses benefit from equal quantity distribution?

  1. Optimized Production Process: Equally distributing the quantity of materials required at each stage ensures a smoother, more streamlined production process. It avoids bottlenecks or unnecessary delays due to waiting for materials to arrive or be produced. 
  2. Reduced Inventory Costs: By planning and managing your materials to be available just when needed, you effectively reduce storage and handling costs. This is because you won't need to hold excess inventory for extended periods. 
  3. Enhanced Quality Control: With the right quantity provisioned when needed, there's a lower risk of errors in production. Hence, overall product quality is likely to improve. 
  4. Greater Customer Satisfaction: Efficient production often leads to timely order fulfillment, which can significantly boost customer satisfaction and loyalty. 
  5. Support for JIT Production: Equal Quantity Distribution aligns well with Just-In-Time (JIT) production, a methodology aimed at reducing waste and enhancing productivity by receiving goods only as they are needed. 

How to set it up? 

The equal distribution function is available in discrete manufacturing, process manufacturing and repetitive manufacturing. You can set it basically at two places: 

  1. On header level of the production version material level or, 
  2. at component level in the bill of material (BOM) 

In addition the Equal Quantity Distribution can be set to be respected in the Available-to-Promise (ATP) by setting it in the Scope of Check. Let us have a look to a simple example in the system:

 

Conclusion 

The principle of Equal Quantity Distribution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition can significantly improve your production process, especially when working with continuous component feed. It can also improve your order fulfillment as supplies can be considered earlier than only at the end of production.  

Implementing Equal Quantity Distribution might require a shift in mindset, but the payoffs could be substantial - from reduced inventory costs to improved product quality and customer satisfaction. 

So don't shy away from exploring this concept further.  

More Information

  • Product Assistance
  • Questions? Ask here

entry.title: Re: IPS | Transformation IAS Groups as condition
entry.content:
While this question has been raised a while ago, in the meantime there is a solution provided.

If you have not migrated to version 2 please follow this guide to move towards the most recent version:

Step 1.

  • Upgrade from ODATA IPS Connector to SCIM IPS Connector with SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite | SAP Help Portal
  • Once completed run a full user sync.

Step 2.

  • Migrating to Permission-Based User Sync for Stories in People Analytics | SAP Help Portal
  • As you followed the other guide first you can skip step 12.
  • Run a full user sync.
    This sync should drop all users in SAC who do not have reporting permission.
    It will keep the users who have reporting permission.

In case there was any user who previously was the author of a report the report will be reassigned to a dummy user and can be assigned by an admin to one of the remaining SAC users.

entry.title: Re: Connect to ABAP environment using RFC
entry.content: Hi Gustaf, you have to add the RFC function module to a communication scenario and maintain a communication arrangement for the scenario in the ABAP environment, as shown in the tutorial that I provided. The arrangement provides the credentials to access the function module, such as host, user name, and password, for instance. Please note, that it is not possible to maintain communication arrangements in the trial version of the ABAP environment. This can be done in a free tier or licensed system. Best regards, Kai

entry.title: Re: How to receive and invoice bitcoin in SAP - Proof of Concept
entry.content:
Hi Hörb,

Thank you for your thoughtful comment and the kind words!

I completely agree with you. Offering a "BTCpayserver" application via BTP would be an excellent addition for SAP products, especially given the growing interest in cryptocurrency payments.

SAP needs to be prepared for possible Central Bank Digital Currencies or Commercial Bank Money Tokens (which are in the works as we speak).

Implementing a solution for managing BTC within SAP, including UTXO management and multi-sig capabilities, would indeed be essential. Do you have an idea of a solution today which provides this? Any project out there? How does that fit into the overall SAP module architecture (Where inside the SAP ecosystem should this take place?)

Your idea of a BTC subledger is also spot-on. Integrating such a feature into financial reporting would allow for accurate valuation of BTC holdings and streamline the reporting process. So far, I only know of thirdparty applications that offer this.

I'd love to hear more about your thoughts on this topic, especially regarding the architecture and features you'd like to see in such a solution!

entry.title: Customer Usage Dashboard 2nd Edition for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In January 2023, we delivered the first version of the Customer Usage Dashboard for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition on SAP for Me. As mentioned in my previous blog, our goal is to help customers monitor their usage and adoption in order to better understand how their end-users are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This, in turn, helps customers to take data-driven decisions based on their own usage, and to better understand how their investment generates value.

What’s new in the second edition of Customer Usage Dashboard?

In the Most Used Apps table, instead of only showing the top 25 most used applications, we now have the full list of used applications from the past 12 months.

You can choose any monthly time interval from the past 12 months to see how many times each application was started by the end-users within this period. You can also compare the usage of different apps using the Application Name filter. The name of the application is also linked to SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library. 

In the Feature Usage (Scope Item Usage) table, we added a new column showing the DDA (Digital Discovery Assessment) selection status. In case you used the DDA tool during the sales cycle to identify the scopes required to fulfill your business needs, this column shows which scope items were “Selected” or “Not Selected”. With this additional information, you can now monitor which of those selected items are activated, as well as monitoring the usage in the last 6 and 12 months. What’s more, the Scope Items are linked to the respective Scope Item definition on the SAP for Me Process Navigator page with information on process documentation, underlying software components, applications, application roles, test scripts along process flows diagrams, integration information, and more.

These changes are all based on your direct feedback. Please continue to share your feedback and experiences with us so we can improve future versions to help you leverage maximum value.

As always, you can also reach out to your Customer Success Manager (CSM) to have a discussion.

Related Blogs:

Do you want to see how your organization is using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition?

An Uber for Engineering?

Your feedback helps to improve SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  

Learn More:

  • SAP for Me @ sap.com
  • SAP for Me @ SAP Community
  • Sign in to SAP for Me
  • SAP ONE Support Launchpad Transition to SAP for Me
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Topic Page
  • Read other SAP S/4HANA Cloud blog posts and follow

entry.title: BTP Alert Notification - how to send email notifications to several recipients
entry.content:
Dear all,

I'm using Alert Notification Service in BTP to send Alerts about JobScheduler Service, and would like to send the alert to several recipients, i believe i can only use one mail per Action. 

Creating a new action i would have to copy the mail subject and body, doesn't make sense, because in the future if need to change the body in one of them i would have to make this change in all recipients.

Is there any other possibility to send the alert to several recipients?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report / PrintOutputController.ModifyPrinterN...
entry.content: Of course I can't rule out a driver problem, but I don't think there are any permission related problems. The error is reproducible with different printer drivers on my development machines and also on my test VMs. I am local admin on all machines and ran VS and our application as admin as well. I just wonder why this problem only occurs with Crystal Reports. (by the way: Everything is fine with Crystal Reports 8.5 either 😉 ) In our case, using other drivers is not an option unfortunately. There are hundreds of customers using our software and we have no control of the printers and printer drivers. I will do more tests using your test application. 🙂

entry.title: API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV - Calling from Build Process Automation/ Build Process
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

we are trying to explore the option of using the API - API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV to create Production Orders for a requirement using Build Process. we are working on S4 Hana on Premise .

we are able to establish the connection from our Sandbox to Build Process and able to call the API - API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV - Reading the Production Order Information using GET Method works fine from Build Process.

for creation of Production Order - we are trying to use the POST Method.

1. Manufacturing Order is mandatory for the API to test it from Build Process even though we are trying to create the Production order. This is not the same if we test the API using Gateway method. I tried to pass some dummy number to this field.

2. Total Quantity field - we are receiving the error. Below is the error which we are receiving when we try to test the POST Method under Actions 

Failed to read property 'TotalQuantity' at offset '81'

 

Really appreciate if you can provide some guidance.

 

Thanks,

Adi

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hello @Udo , Thanks for the response. 

Configuration was missing in SPRO-Sales and Distribution-sales-Advance Return Management-Rename Follow up activity- 
After i renamed and Maintained-- 0027- Continue Inhouse repair. saved. 


Availibility was showing as Delivered at Repair Center. (1. GR. 2. Material Inspection-- Follow up activtiy as 0027- 0027- Continue Inhouse repair) 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV - Calling from Build Proce...
Fiori Tools Visual Studio Code Completion No Suggestions
entry.content:
Hi,

when trying to develop a Fiori Elements App in Fiori Tools for Visual Studio there are almost never any suggestions for values. What I really want to find out for example which options there are for the chart settings like here when developing an Analytical List Page:

There are no suggestions on what values I can put in here, I guess because they are strings(?).
When i go to the API reference for example for sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage I would expect maybe a list of settings possible for this, but there isn't. Is there a way to find out what can be done within the settings of those completely SAP standard elements? So far I always just found the options through scattered documentation by SAP or by blog posts. 

I installed the extensions the sap recommends for visual studio. 
 
To be honest it is a bit frustrating to have to test where for example if it is called "
presentationAnnotationPath" or just "annotationPath" or anything else, with no way for me to find out. Like in the backend CDS where you can see what are the possibilities, and test around.
 
Sorry if I came across a little harsh. 
 
Kind Regards and thank you very much, 
Stephan

entry.title: Re: Java Development in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)
entry.content:
try to add the proxy config to your settings.xml might work.

 

 <proxies>
   <proxy>
      <id>example-proxy</id>
      <active>true</active>
      <protocol>http</protocol>
      <host>localhost</host>
      <port>8887</port>      
    </proxy>
  </proxies>

entry.title: Re: Reversal of PI Count Document
entry.content:
There is no possibility to reverse/cancel the PI Count document once it is Finished.

It is recommended to perform Adhoc count (Inventory Adjustment) to rectify the issue for the required Key Dates.

entry.title: Re: How to Store API Tokens in SAP ABAP?

And the key has again to be stored somewhere.
